audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim assembly, camp, escaped-slave, sexual-cult, interest, vow, and neighbor-harvest law, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for genital-status and mamzer exclusions, Ammon/Moab restrictions, Edom/Egypt inclusion, camp emissions and sanitation, escaped-slave asylum, cult-prostitution language, interest, vows, and limited access to neighboring crops.

verses.restored: One wounded by crushing of testicles or whose male organ is cut shall not enter assembly of YHWH.
A mamzer/person of prohibited union shall not enter assembly of YHWH; even tenth generation from him shall not enter assembly of YHWH.
An Ammonite or Moabite shall not enter assembly of YHWH; even tenth generation from them shall not enter assembly of YHWH forever,
because they did not meet you with bread and water on way when you came out from Egypt, and because he hired against you Balaam son of Beor from Pethor of Aram-naharaim to curse you.
But YHWH your Divine was unwilling to listen to Balaam, and YHWH your Divine turned curse into blessing for you, because YHWH your Divine loved you.
You shall not seek their peace or their prosperity all your days forever.
You shall not abhor an Edomite, for he is your brother; you shall not abhor an Egyptian, for you were a sojourner in his land.
Children born to them in third generation may enter assembly of YHWH.
When you go out as camp against your enemies, guard yourself from every evil thing.
If there is among you a man who is not clean because of occurrence at night, he shall go outside camp; he shall not enter midst of camp.
Toward turning of evening he shall wash in water, and when sun comes he may enter midst of camp.
A place shall be for you outside camp, and there you shall go out.
A peg shall be for you with your equipment; when you sit outside, you shall dig with it and turn and cover your excrement.
For YHWH your Divine walks in midst of your camp to rescue you and give your enemies before you; your camp shall be holy, so he sees no naked/indecent thing among you and turns away from you.
You shall not deliver to his master a servant who escapes to you from his master.
With you he shall dwell, in your midst, in place he chooses in one of your gates, where it is good for him; you shall not oppress him.
There shall not be a female cult-prostitute from daughters of Israel, and there shall not be a male cult-prostitute from sons of Israel.
You shall not bring hire of a prostitute or price of a dog/male prostitute into house of YHWH your Divine for any vow, for both of them are an abomination to YHWH your Divine.
You shall not charge interest to your brother—interest of silver, interest of food, interest of anything lent at interest.
To foreigner you may charge interest, but to your brother you shall not charge interest, so YHWH your Divine may bless you in every undertaking of your hand upon land where you enter to possess it.
When you vow a vow to YHWH your Divine, you shall not delay to fulfill it, for YHWH your Divine will surely require it from you, and sin/misalignment would be in you.
But if you refrain from vowing, sin/misalignment shall not be in you.
What goes out from your lips you shall keep and do, according to what you vowed to YHWH your Divine as freewill offering, which you spoke with your mouth.
When you enter vineyard of your neighbor, you may eat grapes according to your soul/life, to your fill, but into your vessel you shall not put any.
When you enter standing grain of your neighbor, you may pluck heads with your hand, but sickle you shall not swing upon standing grain of your neighbor.

translation_notes: Assembly exclusions based on genital injury and birth status are ancient purity/kinship rules that must not be applied to eunuchs, intersex people, transgender people, infertile people, adopted people, or children born outside approved unions. Isaiah 56 later explicitly imagines faithful eunuchs receiving an enduring name.
Mamzer is a technical and debated status term, not a general insult equivalent to modern 'illegitimate child.' No child bears moral shame for circumstances of birth.
Ammonite and Moabite collective exclusion stands in tension with other biblical traditions, especially Ruth the Moabite, and must not support ethnic hatred.
The camp rules combine ritual status with practical sanitation. Nocturnal emission is not moral guilt.
Verses 15-16 provide extraordinary asylum to an escaped slave: the person must not be returned, may choose where to live, and may not be oppressed.
Qadesh/qedeshah refers to cultic sexual roles, though exact historical practice is debated. The language must not be used to demean sex workers or LGBTQ people.
The in-group/foreigner interest distinction reflects unequal ancient economics and cannot justify predatory lending to immigrants or outsiders.
Neighbor-crop access permits immediate hunger relief while limiting commercial harvesting.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 23 to exclude people because of genitals, infertility, gender identity, ancestry, birth circumstances, ethnicity, sexual history, or disability. Never return an escaped person to slavery or abuse. Center asylum, chosen residence, non-oppression, honest vows, protection from predatory interest, sanitation, and limited food access for the hungry.
